req.title: Biller registration
req.statement: A customer shall register a biller only after a 24-hour hold; withdrawals to new billers shall be deferred.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-023 mandates a 24-hour hold on newly registered billers and deferred withdrawals. Four tests are designed: (1) EP positive — a valid new biller registration returns 201 with hold state; (2) NEG negative — duplicate biller registration returns 409; (3) BVA boundary — a withdrawal attempted exactly at T+0 (before hold expires) is deferred/rejected; (4) STC state transition — after the 24-hour hold elapses the biller transitions to active and withdrawals are permitted. Tests are self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up billers, and use time-travel/mock helpers to control clock state.

tests.name: test_register_new_biller_enters_hold_state
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid JWT in auth_headers fixture)
Biller with the test account number does not exist for this customer
actions: POST /billers with a valid biller payload
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains status field equal to 'hold'
Response body contains hold_expires_at timestamp ~24 h in the future
trace: R-FIN-023: biller in hold state after registration
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_register_new_biller_enters_hold_state(auth_headers, api_client, unique_biller_payload, cleanup_biller):
    payload = unique_biller_payload()
    resp = api_client.post("/billers", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    cleanup_biller(payload["account_number"])

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "hold"

    hold_expires_at = datetime.fromisoformat(body["hold_expires_at"])
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    expected_expiry = now + timedelta(hours=24)
    delta = abs((hold_expires_at - expected_expiry).total_seconds())
    assert delta < 60, f"hold_expires_at not ~24 h from now: {hold_expires_at}"

tests.name: test_register_duplicate_biller_returns_409
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid JWT in auth_headers fixture)
A biller with the same account number already exists for this customer
actions: POST /billers with biller payload for account that already exists
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an error code indicating duplicate biller
trace: R-FIN-023: error pathway — 409 if duplicate biller
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_register_duplicate_biller_returns_409(auth_headers, api_client, existing_biller_payload, cleanup_biller):
    payload = existing_biller_payload()
    # First registration (seed)
    seed = api_client.post("/billers", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert seed.status_code in (201, 409), "Seed step failed unexpectedly"

    # Duplicate attempt
    resp = api_client.post("/billers", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    cleanup_biller(payload["account_number"])

    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "code" in body
    error_text = str(body.get("error", body.get("code", ""))).lower()
    assert "duplicate" in error_text or "conflict" in error_text or "exists" in error_text

tests.name: test_withdrawal_deferred_before_hold_expires
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid JWT in auth_headers fixture)
A biller has just been registered (T+0, hold not yet expired)
Clock has NOT been advanced past 24 hours
actions: POST /billers with a valid biller payload to obtain a biller_id in hold state
Immediately POST /withdrawals targeting the new biller_id
Capture withdrawal response status and body
expected: Withdrawal response is HTTP 202 with status 'deferred' OR HTTP 403/422 indicating withdrawal blocked during hold
trace: R-FIN-023: withdrawals to new billers shall be deferred
R-FIN-023: 24-hour hold boundary — T+0 is inside hold window

tests.name: test_biller_transitions_to_active_after_24h_hold
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid JWT in auth_headers fixture)
A biller was registered and is in hold state
Clock or mock is advanced past 24 hours (hold_expires_at is in the past)
actions: POST /billers to register a new biller (biller enters hold state)
Advance system clock past 24 hours using advance_clock helper
POST /withdrawals targeting the biller to confirm it is accepted
trace: R-FIN-023: 24-hour hold; after hold expires biller becomes usable
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_biller_transitions_to_active_after_24h_hold(
    auth_headers, api_client, unique_biller_payload, cleanup_biller, advance_clock
):
    payload = unique_biller_payload()
    reg = api_client.post("/billers", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert reg.status_code == 201
    biller_id = reg.json()["id"]
    assert reg.json()["status"] == "hold"

    advance_clock(hours=24, biller_id=biller_id)

    get_resp = api_client.get(f"/billers/{biller_id}", headers=auth_headers)
    cleanup_biller(payload["account_number"])
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "active"

    withdrawal_payload = {"biller_id": biller_id, "amount": "1.00", "currency": "USD"}
    w_resp = api_client.post("/withdrawals", json=withdrawal_payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert w_resp.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Expected withdrawal accepted after hold, got {w_resp.status_code}"
    )
